data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: SMCI, AMD, SONY, and more
data.description:
Stock futures held steady on Wednesday, following a mixed session, as investors assessed easing trade tensions and broader market signals.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) +7% — Shares rose after Raymond James initiated coverage with an “Outperform” rating and $41 PT, highlighting the AI server maker as a "near AI pure play." Analyst Simon Leopold noted AI platforms make up ~70% of SMCI’s revenue, with the company capturing 31% of the branded AI server market. Despite near-term risks tied to tariffs and tech transitions (e.g., NVIDIA's Hopper to Blackwell), long-term AI demand remains a strong growth driver. Leopold sees SMCI well-positioned between traditional IT giants and contract manufacturers, offering customized solutions with scaled manufacturing. Key catalysts include hyperscale AI growth, NVIDIA’s new platform ramp, and enterprise penetration in verticals like finance and healthcare.
  • Sony (NYSE:SONY) +6% — Shares rose after the company announced a ¥250B ($1.7B) share buyback and reported Q4 operating income of ¥203.6B, topping estimates of ¥192.2B despite an 11% Y/Y decline. Sony also unveiled plans for a partial spinoff of its financial unit, with 80% of shares to be distributed to shareholders via dividends; the unit will be classified as a discontinued operation from this quarter. However, the rally was tempered by a disappointing outlook for FY2026, as the company flagged a ¥100B ($700M) hit from U.S. tariffs, guiding to flat operating profit of ¥1.28T, below the ¥1.5T analyst consensus.
  • AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) +6% — Shares soared after the company announced a new $6B share buyback program, supplementing the $4B remaining under its previous authorization as of March 29. “Our expanded share repurchase program reflects the board’s confidence in AMD’s strategic direction, growth prospects, and ability to consistently generate strong free cash flow,” said Chair and CEO Dr. Lisa Su, emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and a commitment to shareholder returns.

Biggest stock losers 

  • Alcon (NYSE:ALC) -7% — Shares dropped after Q1 results and updated guidance fell short of expectations, with the company flagging a gross tariff impact of ~$80M, expected to pressure sales and costs. While Alcon aims to offset the tariff hit via operational and FX actions, its revised outlook reflects dilutive effects from recent deals, weighing on core operating margin by ~80 bps and EPS by ~$0.10. FY2025 net sales outlook was raised to $10.4–$10.5B (from $10.2–$10.4B), but core op. margin was trimmed to 20–21% (from 21–22%), and core EPS was cut to $3.05–$3.15 (vs. prior $3.15–$3.25 and consensus $3.24).
  • American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) -15% — Shares slumped after the company pulled its full-year forecast, citing macro uncertainty, and said it is “actively evaluating” its forward plans. For Q1, AEO expects revenue to decline 5% to ~$1.1B (vs. $1.09B consensus) and anticipates a GAAP operating loss of ~$85M and an adjusted loss of ~$68M. The results reflect higher-than-expected promotional activity, a $75M inventory write-down on spring and summer merchandise, and a $17M restructuring charge tied to the closure of two fulfillment centers. Comparable sales are projected to fall ~3%, with American Eagle down 2% and Aerie down 4%. CEO Jay Schottenstein acknowledged merchandising missteps, but noted inventory is now better aligned heading into Q2.
  • Aurora Innovation (NASDAQ:AUR) -13% — Shares plummeted after Uber (NYSE:UBER) announced a $1B private offering of Exchangeable Senior Notes due 2028, initially convertible into Aurora stock at an implied price of ~$8.50 per share. The structure sparked investor concerns about potential dilution, as each $1,000 note is exchangeable into 117.6471 shares. Uber, which acquired a 26% stake in Aurora in December 2020 as part of the sale of its autonomous driving unit, Advanced Technologies Group, and has since held board representation through CEO Dara Khosrowshahi, said it may use the proceeds—estimated at up to $1.13B—for general corporate purposes, including strategic investments.
  • Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) -4% — Shares fell after Jefferies downgraded the stock to Hold from Buy, pointing to weakening demand and a lack of near-term catalysts, such as progress updates on the R2 model. While the firm acknowledged Rivian’s brand strength and software capabilities, it flagged the need for the EV maker to demonstrate sustainable gross margin and greater capital efficiency. Jefferies cut its 2025 revenue forecast on lower unit sales expectations but expects Rivian to remain funded through the R2 launch in H1 2026, with $1.5B in net liquidity by year-end. The firm set a $16 price target.

data.title: Informatica unveils agentic AI offerings; collaborations with Microsoft, others
data.description:
Informatica made a flurry of announcements at its Informatica World event in Las Vegas about its suite of AI-powered products and several collaborations, including one with Microsoft (MSFT).

Informatica AI agents:

Informatica unveiled agentic AI offerings on Its AI-powered cloud data management platform.

The company unveiled AI Agent Engineering — a new service that enables enterprises to build, connect and manage intelligent AI agent workflows. AI Agent Engineering is expected to be available globally in fall of 2025 as part of Informatica’s cloud data management platform.

Informatica also launched Claire Agents, a suite of autonomous digital assistants designed to augment enterprise data management. The Claire Agents are expected to be available in preview starting in the fall of 2025, according to the company.

Informatica said its strategic approach to Agentic AI expands on its AI innovation history, which includes the launch of  Claire GPT,  Claire  Copilot and GenAI blueprints for major cloud ecosystem partners.

The company added that Claire Copilot is generally available for data integration and cloud application integration, starting May.

"With the launch of CLAIRE® Agents and AI Agent Engineering, we are redefining what is possible in data management and AI orchestration," said Informatica CEO Amit Walia.

Informatica and Oracle platform:

Separately, Informatica noted the upcoming availability of its Master Data Management, or MDM, service and Intelligent Data Management Cloud platform service on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, or OCI (ORCL), enabling customers to use Informatica MDM natively in their OCI environment.

The company said it has been designated as a preferred partner for enterprise cloud data integration, data governance and MDM for data warehouse and lakehouse solutions on OCI. 

MDM service helps unify data across an enterprise. The availability of Informatica MDM software as a service, or SaaS, on OCI enables companies to create a single and unified view of their business data, according to the company.

Informatica and Microsoft:

Informatica announced a strategic agreement with Microsoft under which the companies will deliver unified, AI-powered solutions that combine Informatica’s Intelligent Data Management Cloud platform with key Microsoft services, including Microsoft Fabric and Microsoft Azure.

Informatica’s cloud data management platform is available as a SaaS offering on Azure and through Azure Marketplace.

"By integrating our cloud data management capabilities directly into Microsoft Fabric, and building CLAIRE Copilots leveraging Azure OpenAI Service, we're empowering business users to build enterprise grade analytics and AI applications,” said Krish Vitaldevara, Chief Product Officer at Informatica.

Informatica and Amazon Bedrock:

Informatica announced "new recipes" to develop AI Agents, built with Amazon Bedrock — an Amazon service that offers foundation models from Amazon and other AI companies, along with tools for developers to create AI applications securely and privately.

Informatica said the service enables joint customers to build, connect, and manage AI agent workflows with trusted data and no-code simplicity.

The company announced two new recipes for AI agents built with Amazon Bedrock: Supply Chain Management and Simple REACT Agent AI are now generally available.

In addition, the company announced the achievement of Amazon Generative AI Competency and innovations across its analytics portfolio for AWS services, including Amazon Redshift and Amazon SageMaker Lakehouse.

Informatica and Salesforce’s Agentforce:

Informatica said it plans to expand its partnership with Salesforce (CRM) to integrate its Intelligent Data Management Cloud platform with Salesforce’s Agentforce — a service which helps users to build, customize, and deploy autonomous AI Agents.

The company unveiled Agentforce for Sales and Agentforce for Service. Informatica MDM SaaS will use records— data collected from several enterprise systems — to enrich AI agents built with Agentforce, supporting critical sales and service use cases across business to customers and business to business environment.

Informatica and Databricks:

Informatica announced an expanded partnership with Databricks under which customers could modernize their on-premises data lakes by combining Informatica’s Intelligent Data Management Cloud platform with Databricks' Data Intelligence Platform to provide a complete data foundation for their future analytics and AI workloads.

data.title: Deere Q2 2025 Earnings Preview: Margin debate takes center stage
data.description:
Deere (NYSE:DE) is scheduled to announce Q2 earnings results on Thursday, May 15th, before market open.

Analysts expect the firm to earn $5.62 on revenue of $10.79B (-29.2% Y/Y).

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 3 upward revisions and 11 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 2 upward revisions and 7 downward.

The negative skew in estimate revisions reflects tempered expectations for the quarter, likely due to softening demand across the agriculture and construction sectors and ongoing destocking by dealers following post-pandemic supply normalization.

Investors will be watching closely for management commentary on order trends and dealer inventory levels, especially in large ag equipment, and any updates to full-year guidance.

According to Oppenheimer, the bigger debate among investors is the margin outlook, which remains surprisingly resilient despite steep production cuts. The firm notes that Deere is still guiding toward a healthy 30% consolidated incremental margin from Q1 to Q2, citing two key drivers: delayed delivery of margin-rich seasonal products, expected to land in Q2 and improving margins in the construction & forestry division.

However, looking beyond Q2, Oppenheimer has moderated its full-year margin outlook due to rising price/cost pressures from tariffs.

data.title: Dutch Bros attracts a bull rating from RBC Capital with the brand gaining popularity with Generation Z
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets started off coverage of Dutch Bros Inc (NYSE:BROS) with an Outperform rating.

Analyst Logan Reich highlighted that Dutch Bros (NYSE:BROS) is a unique brand and model in the coffee/beverage space, which resonates with Gen Z consumers. The company is seen having multiple top-line catalysts that could drive upside to the BROS sales targets for the next few years. "Further, it is still in the relatively early stages of a national expansion with unit growth opportunity in new & existing markets which should support a mid-teens growth rate for the foreseeable future," wrote Reich.

Dutch Bros (BROS) was called differentiated from large incumbent coffee chains Starbucks (SBUX) and Dunkin' Donuts, which represented 59% of industry sales last year. It was also noted that the BROS management team is strong, sets achievable expectations, and communicates clearly with investors.

Shares of Dutch Bros (BROS) are up 34.0% on a year-to-date basis, which ranks 2nd out of the 54 publicly traded restaurant stocks. Short interest on BROS stands at 4.6% of the total float.

data.title: Owens Corning announces up to 12M share buyback program
data.description:
Building products company Owens Corning (NYSE:OC) announced on Wednesday a new stock repurchase authorization for up to 12M shares of common stock.

Shares were 0.92% higher during morning trade to $143.92.

The new authorization comes in addition to the previously announced share repurchase program, under which about 5.7M shares remained available for buyback as of March 31.

The company may repurchase shares through open-market, privately negotiated, or other transactions.

Additionally, Owens Corning announced the hosting of its 2025 Investor Day from 9 AM ET to approximately 12 PM ET.

data.title: Alico raises 2025 land sales outlook to over $50M as strategic transformation accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Alico, Inc. (ALCO) Q2 2025

Management View

  • John Kiernan, President and CEO, reported significant progress in Alico’s Strategic Transformation announced in January, highlighting the completion of the fiscal year 2025 harvest and the effective conclusion of most capital investment in Citrus Operations. He stated, "We will conduct a final harvest on the majority of the remaining 3,783 acres of operational citrus groves in fiscal year 2026." Workforce reductions from approximately 200 to 25 employees were cited as aligning the organization with the new business model and lowering operating expenses.
  • Kiernan announced, "We are now raising our outlook to potentially have an additional $30 million of land sales or more this fiscal year, which would be a 150% increase from our prior guidance for fiscal year 2025 expected land sales. This acceleration in land sales could dramatically improve our annual adjusted EBITDA and strengthen our ability to return capital to shareholders."
  • He discussed diversification efforts, mentioning agreements to lease approximately 5,250 acres to third-party citrus growers next season and ongoing discussions for seasonal crops, sod production, and sand mining. Entitlement work for development properties, led by EVP Mitch Hutchcraft, is advancing, with the Corkscrew Grove Villages development application representing a major milestone.
  • The CEO highlighted a new $50 million share repurchase program and a recently amended credit agreement, which reduces insurance requirements and offers cost savings and greater flexibility.
  • Brad Heine, CFO, noted, "Revenue decreased 1% to $18 million, compared to $18.1 million for the prior year period." He also referenced lower harvest volumes due to Hurricane Milton, improved pricing from a Tropicana contract, and a major increase in land management and other operations revenue, driven by rock and sand royalties and sod sales.
  • Heine stated, "Total operating expenses...were $167.7 million...compared to $36.3 million in the same period in the prior year. The increase...was driven by approximately $118 million of non-cash accelerated depreciation as a result of our Strategic Transformation and the decision to wind down our Citrus Operations, as well as the impairment of our young trees...of $25 million."

Outlook

  • Kiernan projected a fiscal year-end cash balance of approximately $25 million, net debt of approximately $60 million, and adjusted EBITDA of about $20 million for fiscal year 2025, supported by $20 million in land sales and citrus harvest proceeds. He added, "We currently project that land sales could potentially exceed $50 million this year, which would increase our adjusted EBITDA and cash and decrease our net debt projections."
  • The company’s near-term focus includes completing identified land sales, advancing entitlement work, finalizing agreements with agricultural operators, and strengthening the balance sheet.

Financial Results

  • For the three months ended March 31, 2025, revenue was $18 million, down 1% from the prior year period. For the six months ended March 31, 2025, revenue was $34.9 million.
  • Citrus harvest volumes were lower due to Hurricane Milton, with 4.7 million pounds solids harvested in the quarter. Blended price per pound solids increased by $0.70 year-over-year.
  • Land Management and Other operations revenue increased 107% in the quarter, attributed to higher rock and sand royalties and sod sales.
  • Operating expenses for the quarter were $167.7 million, driven by $118 million in non-cash accelerated depreciation and $25 million in asset impairment.
  • The reported net loss attributable to common stockholders for the quarter was $111.4 million, with a loss of $14.58 per diluted share. EBITDA was a loss of $14.7 million, but adjusted EBITDA was a gain of $12.7 million.
  • Cash and cash equivalents stood at $14.7 million, with net cash used in operating activities at $0.6 million for the six months ended March 31, 2025. Net debt was $74.9 million at quarter end.

Q&A

  • Brandon Rogers, ROTH Capital: Asked for color on the $15.8 million landfill in the quarter and the number and location of acres. Brad Heine responded, "2,100 -- that was 2,100 acres." Rogers followed up on the county location, and Heine answered, "Hendry County."
  • Rogers inquired about confidence in achieving $50 million in land sales. Kiernan explained, "We’ve negotiated an agreement to sell some acres...it’s going to go through diligence right now...timing is a bit uncertain...we’re talking to several other parties about potential land sales, but nothing that’s solid enough for us to report at this time."
  • On Corkscrew Village milestones, Rogers asked about what to watch for. Kiernan said, "I think the entitlement approvals themselves are kind of what the milestones would be...as you see the approvals at the local, state and federal levels come through, you’ll know where the milestones stand."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst questions focused on transaction details, sale timing, and project milestones, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone given the company’s proactive responses and clear strategic direction.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat sentiment in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing progress and flexibility: "I’m pleased with the progress we’ve made in positioning Alico for sustainable long-term growth." There were no instances of defensiveness or hesitation.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts displayed greater confidence and focus on forward-looking opportunities, while the prior quarter was more cautious due to ongoing operational transitions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw an increase in land sales outlook from $20 million to potentially over $50 million, a new $50 million share repurchase program, and the near completion of the citrus operation wind-down, compared to the previous quarter’s initial transformation announcement.
  • Strategic focus shifted further toward land monetization and development entitlement, with more specifics provided on Corkscrew Grove Villages and other properties.
  • Analysts this quarter were more focused on execution and timing, rather than the viability of the transformation strategy, reflecting increased clarity and confidence from management.
  • Financials this quarter showed higher reported losses due to non-cash charges, but adjusted EBITDA improved materially from the previous period.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the uncertainty and timing of pending land sales as a key challenge, noting, "there is no certainty regarding timing until sales are closed."
  • The impact of Hurricane Milton continued to affect harvest volumes and revenues.
  • The company is mitigating risks through diversified agricultural operations, adjusted credit agreements, and an expanded capital allocation strategy including share repurchases and debt reduction.
  • Analyst questions reflected concerns about the certainty and timing of land sales and entitlement approvals.

Final Takeaway

Alico, Inc. emphasized its accelerated transformation to a diversified land company, underscored by a substantial increase in the land sales outlook and the wind-down of citrus operations. Management highlighted the strengthening of the balance sheet, a new share repurchase program, and the advancement of development entitlements as drivers of potential long-term shareholder value. The company projects further improvements in adjusted EBITDA and net debt should additional land sales close this year, supporting its strategy to unlock the value of its real estate portfolio.

data.title: Incyte and Novartis settle royalties dispute related to Jakafi
data.description:
Incyte (NASDAQ:INCY) and Novartis AG (NYSE:NVS) reached a settlement regarding royalty payments in an existing collaboration between the companies for blood-cancer treatment Jakafi.

In a filing on Wednesday, Incyte said that it agreed to pay Novartis $280M as part of the disputed royalties on U.S. sales of Jakafi. The companies also settled to reduce by 50% the royalty rate payable by Incyte on future sales of Jakafi in the U.S. beginning January 1, 2025.

Incyte said that this would lead to reduced royalty payable for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, to about $14.9M. 

The difference of $242.2M between the total accrued royalties and the total amount to be paid to Novartis, will be recorded in Incyte's results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025.

data.title: CAE targets 150% cash conversion and mid to high single digit Civil operating income growth for 2026 amid record $20.1B backlog
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CAE Inc. (CAE) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Marc Parent, President and CEO, stated that "We delivered an exceptional fourth quarter that capped a strong year across all of our key financial and operational metrics," highlighting $289 million in free cash flow for the quarter and a record $814 million for the full year, with a cash conversion rate of 211%. He emphasized that this enabled CAE to meet its year-end leverage target and provided increased flexibility for future growth.

  • Parent reported building momentum for long-term growth and profitability, securing $1.3 billion in new orders in the quarter and ending the year with a record adjusted backlog of $20.1 billion, up 65% from last year. Civil segment backlog grew 37% to a record $8.8 billion, supported by $3.7 billion in new orders, including 56 full flight simulators.

  • Parent noted that Defense segment adjusted backlog nearly doubled to $11.3 billion, with $596 million in quarterly orders and a record $4 billion for the year. He cited strong program execution and market tailwinds as key drivers.

  • Constantino Malatesta, Interim CFO, stated, "Revenue of $1.3 billion was up 13% compared to the fourth quarter last year. Adjusted segment operating income was $258.8 million compared to $125.7 million last year. Quarterly EPS was $0.47 per share compared to $0.12 in the fourth quarter last year."

  • Malatesta added, "Net cash provided by operating activities was $322.7 million for the quarter compared to $216.2 million in the fourth quarter of last year. For the year, we generated $896.5 million from operating activities compared to $566.9 million last year."

  • Parent also announced a planned CEO transition later in the summer, with the Board in the process of identifying the next CEO and committing to a seamless handover.

Outlook

  • Parent stated, "We expect segment operating income to grow in the mid to high single digit percentage range, with a modest increase in the annual adjusted segment operating income margin" for Civil in fiscal 2026. He also indicated that the Defense segment is expected to have low double digit percentage annual segment operating income growth and an annual segment operating margin in the 8% to 8.5% range.

  • Management expects a cash conversion rate of 150% for fiscal 2026 and beyond, representing a significant increase from the previous 100% target.

  • Regarding capital expenditures, Malatesta said, "We remain highly focused on capital efficiency and expect total CapEx in fiscal 2026 to be modestly lower than in fiscal 2025."

Financial Results

  • Consolidated revenue was $1.3 billion for the quarter, up 13% year-over-year. Adjusted segment operating income was $258.8 million, and EPS was $0.47 per share. For the full year, consolidated revenue was $4.7 billion, and adjusted segment operating income was $732 million. Annual adjusted net income was $385.5 million or $1.21 per share.

  • Free cash flow for the quarter was $289 million, a record $814 million for the year, and net cash provided by operating activities for the quarter was $322.7 million.

  • Civil fourth-quarter revenue was $728.4 million, and adjusted segment operating income was $208.4 million, with a margin of 28.6%. Civil delivered 15 full flight simulators in the quarter and 61 for the year. Defense fourth-quarter revenue was $547 million, and adjusted segment operating income was $50.4 million, with a 9.2% margin.

  • Net debt at the end of the quarter was $3.2 billion, with a net debt to adjusted EBITDA of 2.77 times.

Q&A

  • Kevin Chiang, CIBC: Asked about the Civil outlook, commercial versus business jet training demand, and margin outlook. Parent responded that CAE is taking a measured approach due to a "more cautious tone that we hear from airlines and a bit of softness that we saw late in fiscal year," noting resilience in training demand and expecting modestly lower simulator deliveries in the first half of the year.

  • Fadi Chamoun, BMO: Inquired about the flight operation business and competitive position. Parent replied, "We're winning in the market... our order book is growing. I can tell you that basically, I would say we're at capacity for the work that we can take on right now."

  • Konark Gupta, Scotiabank: Sought clarity on defense margin guidance. Parent explained, "We're not putting a lid on anything... we're ramping up higher margin programs that we won in committed backlog." Malatesta added, "We are aiming to take our leverage down to 2.5 times by the end of the year."

  • Cameron Doerksen, National Bank Financial: Asked about CapEx, with Malatesta noting, "Total CapEx expected to be modestly lower than last year... 25% maintenance CapEx." Parent emphasized the shift from a multiyear investment cycle to "consolidating and operationalizing that opportunity."

  • Greg Konrad, Jefferies: Queried Civil backlog conversion. Andrew Arnovitz indicated that consolidation of the SimCom JV and Flexjet contract, which is spread over 15 years, impacts year-one revenue recognition.

  • Tim James, TD Cowen: Asked about Canadian defense opportunities. Parent stated, "I feel very, very good that, that's going to translate into significant growth for CAE on top of the already very significant level of order intake and actually revenue that we're generating right now from contracts in Canada."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts adopted a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on margin outlooks, backlog conversion, and the strategic position in both Civil and Defense. Questions were probing but did not reflect heightened skepticism.

  • Management's tone was confident and optimistic in prepared remarks, with Parent stating, "With continued focus on execution, innovation and value creation, I'm confident that we'll build on this foundation to deliver growth, higher margins and stronger free cash flow." During Q&A, responses were more measured and occasionally cautious, reflecting careful optimism amid macro uncertainty.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a similar level of confidence but provided more specific guidance and acknowledged first-half caution in Civil.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Civil segment operating income shifted from "modestly below our previous outlook" last quarter to "mid to high single digit percentage range" growth this quarter, with a "modest increase" in margin.

  • Defense margin guidance evolved from "modestly above previously indicated range of 6% to 7%" to "8% to 8.5%" for the year ahead.

  • Management's tone remained confident, but with greater detail on cash conversion targets and capital allocation discipline. Analysts' focus shifted toward backlog conversion, margin sustainability, and capital efficiency.

  • Strategic focus moved further towards capital efficiency, deleveraging, and operationalizing growth investments, with less emphasis on new capacity expansion.

Risks and Concerns

  • Malatesta noted higher finance expense resulting from lease expenses and SIMCOM joint venture consolidation. Income tax expense increased, with an effective tax rate of 25% for the quarter versus 14% last year.

  • Parent discussed ongoing OEM supply chain constraints impacting aircraft deliveries and simulator demand, with expectations for "modestly lower simulator deliveries this fiscal year with a greater proportion occurring in the second half."

  • Analysts raised concerns about the timing of backlog conversion, margin sustainability in Defense, and macroeconomic uncertainty in Civil.

Final Takeaway

CAE enters fiscal 2026 with strong momentum, a record $20.1 billion backlog, and clear targets for growth in both Civil and Defense segments. Management projects mid to high single digit Civil operating income growth and low double digit Defense growth, with a focus on higher cash conversion, capital efficiency, and further deleveraging. Strategic priorities are centered on operational execution, resilience in core markets, and disciplined use of capital, positioning CAE for continued expansion and value creation for shareholders.
